title: Inert Prime Area Quantization PASS: p=3, k=3 (mod 27) — first genuine generalization
_fixture_purpose: Genuine generalization step. p=3, k=3, modulus=27. The theorem predicts forbidden = {r : 3|r but 9∤r} = {3,6,12,15,21,24} (six residues with v_3=1 in Z/27Z). The spectrum has 21 elements out of 27, consistent with the pattern: |forbidden| = p^(k-1)*(p-1) = 9*2 = 18... wait, actually |{r : v_p(r)=1, 0<=r<p^k}| = p^(k-1) - p^(k-2) for k>=2? For k=3: {3,6,12,15,21,24} = 6 = p*(p-1) = 3*2 = 6. Correct. The obstruction count grows with k, but the forbidden set remains precisely the v_p=1 residues.

inert_witness.irreducibility_check: x^2+x-1 mod 3: x=0 -> 2, x=1 -> 1, x=2 -> 2. No roots mod 3. Irreducible. (Same prime as anchor; inert status is a property of p, independent of k.)
inert_witness.notes: Legendre(5,3)=-1 (same as p3_k2 anchor). Z[phi]/27Z[phi] = Z[phi]/(3^3 Z[phi]); since 3 is inert, this is Z/27Z[omega] where omega^2+omega-1=0 — the Galois ring GR(27,2).

theorem_claim.statement: For p=3 (inert in Z[phi]) and k=3: Im(f) = {r in Z/27Z : v_3(r) != 1}. Forbidden = {r : 3|r but 9∤r} = {3,6,12,15,21,24}.
theorem_claim.forbidden_description: Residues r in {0..26} with v_3(r)=1: {3,6,12,15,21,24}. These are the six multiples of 3 that are not multiples of 9. The obstruction propagates from Z/9Z to Z/27Z: lifting a forbidden residue r=3u (gcd(u,3)=1) to Z/27Z produces 3u, 3u+9, 3u+18 — all forbidden, consistent with v_3=1 characterization.

validation_checks.description: p=3 is inert in Z[phi]: Legendre(5,3)=-1
